summary_translated: 近期大型语言模型（LLMs）的进步使得高质量散文生成成为可能，但这些模型是否具备生成多样化输出的能力仍存在争议。本文通过叙事相似性框架探究LLM生成故事的多样性。利用来自r/WritingPrompts的人类写作故事及提示数据集，结合对比框架与三种自动标注方法，我们收集了10个代表性LLM的叙事相似性判断结果，涵盖人工评估与自动化评价。研究发现存在一致趋势：LLM生成的叙事彼此之间比人类写作的故事更为相似。我们进一步证明，前沿模型尤其趋向于一个‘平均’的通用叙事，该叙事虽能近似个体人类故事，却缺乏人类作者群体的整体多样性。最后，我们表明常见的缓解策略（如负向提示和温度缩放）无法有效解决这种同质化问题。

summary: Word2Vec's effectiveness at generating semantic embeddings has been widely validated, yet it has been tested almost exclusively on languages with large vocabulary inventories. This study examines whether Word2Vec can successfully capture semantic relationships within an extremely reduced vocabulary using data from Toki Pona, a constructed language with approximately 130 words. We sourced 1.4 million sentences (7.95 million tokens) from the Toki Pona community for training. Approximately 23% of sentences in the corpus contain non-Toki Pona tokens such as named entities, loanwords, and neologisms. To investigate whether this linguistic noise enhances or hinders performance -- a topic rarely addressed in word embedding literature -- we trained two distinct models: one retaining these incidental tokens and another filtering them out completely. Evaluation was conducted using quantitative methods measuring word proximity to semantic category centroids, automated silhouette scores via agglomerative clustering, and qualitative analysis utilizing representational similarity matrices compared against English. The results indicate that while sparse, non-core tokens do not affect the relative structure of the learned embeddings, they actually draw similar words closer together in the vector space. Importantly, Word2Vec's effectiveness depends more on distributional patterns than lexicon size even at this extreme lower bound.
title: Examining the Limits of Word2Vec with Toki Pona
comment: 10 pages, 4 figures, 3 tables. Accepted to the Society for Computation in Linguistics (SCiL) 2026
title_translated: Word2Vec极限探查：基于通天语的研究
summary_translated: Word2Vec在生成语义嵌入方面的有效性已得到广泛验证，但其测试几乎仅限于词汇量庞大的语言。本研究探讨在极小词汇量语言——通天语（Toki Pona，约130个词）中，Word2Vec能否成功捕捉语义关系。我们从通天语社区获取了140万句（795万词元）文本用于训练。语料库中约23%的句子包含非通天语词元，如专有名词、借词和新造词。为探究此类语言噪声是否促进或阻碍性能——这一在词嵌入文献中极少被讨论的问题，我们训练了两个不同模型：一个保留这些偶然词元，另一个则完全过滤掉。评估采用定量方法，包括词语与语义类别中心点的距离测量、基于凝聚聚类的自动轮廓分数，以及与英语对比的表示相似性矩阵进行定性分析。结果表明，尽管非核心词元稀疏，但它们并未破坏学习到的嵌入相对结构，反而使相似词语在向量空间中更接近。重要的是，Word2Vec的有效性更多依赖于分布模式而非词典规模，即使在极端低词汇量条件下亦然。

summary: While Large Language Model (LLM) based Automatic Speech Recognition (ASR) enables seamless multilingual use, models often misidentify the output language, compromising transcription fidelity and downstream application quality. To preserve flexibility and code-switching capabilities, we propose a soft prompting approach that hints at potential spoken languages without strictly constraining the output. We formally define this challenge as a lack of language adherence, introduce a novel metric to quantify violations, and evaluate three mitigation strategies: (1) zero-shot prompting for robust guidance under uncertainty, (2) supervised fine-tuning (SFT) to improve prompt adherence, and (3) Chain-of-Thought (CoT) reasoning to enforce adherence during decoding. We present a comparative analysis of these methods across multiple languages, evaluating effectiveness in reducing the language violation while maintaining overall ASR performance. Finally, we discuss trade-offs to guide strategy selection under various compute constraints.
title: Are you speaking my languages? On spoken language adherence in multimodal LLMs
comment: 7 pages, 3 tables in the main body
title_translated: 你在说我的语言吗？关于多模态大模型中口语语言一致性的问题
summary_translated: 尽管基于大语言模型（LLM）的自动语音识别（ASR）实现了无缝多语言使用，但模型常出现输出语言误识别问题，损害转录准确性和下游应用质量。为保持灵活性和代码切换能力，我们提出一种软提示方法，通过暗示潜在口语语言而不严格限制输出。我们正式将此挑战定义为语言一致性缺失，引入一种新型度量指标来量化违规情况，并评估三种缓解策略：(1) 零样本提示以在不确定性下提供稳健引导；(2) 监督微调（SFT）以提升提示遵循度；(3) 思维链（CoT）推理以在解码过程中强制语言一致性。我们在多种语言上对这些方法进行了比较分析，评估其在降低语言错误率的同时维持整体ASR性能的效果。最后，我们讨论了不同计算约束下的权衡，以指导策略选择。

summary: This work describes the participation of the MLLP-VRAIN research group in the shared task of the IWSLT 2026 Simultaneous Speech Translation track. Our submission utilizes the recently released Parakeet and Qwen 3.5 models to create a robust, cascaded solution for long-form SimulST through the use of adaptive "black-box" policies. We explore relaxations of these policies to achieve better quality-latency trade-offs. Compared to last year, we participate on all language directions. In addition to this, for the En$\rightarrow${De, It, Zh} directions we also participate in this year's new context track employing a combination of ASR word-boosting and a RAG mechanism of offline pre-translated exemplars to guide generation and enrich our system with domain-specific context. Finally, we provide a detailed latency analysis of our system. Compared to last year, results on the MCIF En$\rightarrow$De test set shows a substantial quality improvement of +5.82 XCOMET-XL. Our context track processing further improves performance by +1.03.
title: MLLP-VRAIN UPV system for the IWSLT 2026 Simultaneous Speech Translation task
title_translated: MLLP-VRAIN UPV系统在IWSLT 2026同步语音翻译任务中的表现
summary_translated: 本文描述了MLLP-VRAIN研究团队参与IWSLT 2026同步语音翻译赛道共享任务的情况。我们的提交方案利用最近发布的Parakeet和Qwen 3.5模型，通过自适应的‘黑盒’策略构建了一个稳健的级联解决方案，用于长篇同步语音翻译（SimulST）。我们探索了这些策略的松弛方式，以实现更好的质量-延迟权衡。相比去年，我们参与了所有语言方向的翻译任务。此外，针对En→De、It、Zh方向，我们还参加了今年新增的上下文赛道，采用ASR词增强与离线预翻译范例的RAG机制相结合的方式，引导生成并丰富系统中的领域特定上下文信息。最后，我们提供了系统的详细延迟分析。相较于去年，MCIF En→De测试集上的结果显示出显著的质量提升，XCOMET-XL得分提高+5.82。上下文赛道处理进一步提升了性能，额外获得+1.03的增益。

summary: The rapid rise in popularity of large language models (LLMs) for translation calls for a thorough study of the reliability of their confidence in their own outputs. Unlike many generation tasks, translation errors and confidence levels can be useful at different levels of granularity (tokens, words, or spans). Unsupervised approaches based on internal signals like predicted probabilities can be misleading because they reflect certainty among alternatives rather than correctness. In addition, they require access to such internal signals. Here, we devise five verbalized methods of extracting an LLM's per-token confidence without those shortcomings and compare their reliability with that of the model's internal signals of certainty. We evaluate reliability using two forms of alignment: fine-grained error detection and calibration. For both, internal and verbalized methods perform similarly, although results vary by model. Interestingly, we find little to no correlation between internal and verbalized methods.
title: Speaking in Self-Assessing Tongues: On the Verbalized Confidence of LLMs in Machine Translation
title_translated: 自评语言中的言语化自信：大语言模型在机器翻译中的自我评估信心研究
summary_translated: 大语言模型（LLMs）在翻译任务中日益流行，亟需对其自身输出的可靠性进行深入研究。与许多生成任务不同，翻译错误和置信度可在不同粒度级别（如词元、单词或短语）上体现。基于内部信号（如预测概率）的无监督方法可能具有误导性，因为它们反映的是在多种选择之间的确定性而非正确性，且需要访问这些内部信号。本文提出五种无需依赖此类缺陷的言语化方法，用于提取LLM对每个词元的置信度，并将其可靠性与模型内部的确定性信号进行比较。通过细粒度错误检测和校准两种对齐方式评估可靠性，结果表明内部方法与言语化方法表现相当，但具体效果因模型而异。有趣的是，我们发现内部方法与言语化方法之间相关性极低甚至没有。

summary: Recent advances in multimodal learning, including large language models (LLMs) and vision-language models (VLMs), have demonstrated strong adaptability to natural images. However, extending their use to the medical domain, particularly for volumetric (3D) images, is challenging due to high computational complexity, volumetric dependencies and the semantic gap between visual features and clinical terminology. Naively fine-tuning LLMs on limited medical data often leads to overfitting and clinical hallucination, where linguistic fluency is prioritized over clinical factuality. In this study, we investigate parameter-efficient adaptation strategies for volumetric CT report generation and introduce RAD3D-Prefix, a lightweight diagnostic-prior conditioning framework that minimizes the need for extensive parameter training. This module integrates image embeddings with multi-label diagnostic classification logits, preserving critical clinical details while bridging the semantic gap. By keeping the LLM frozen, our method requires minimal trainable parameters and mitigates the risk of overfitting on small, domain-specific datasets. Through a systematic study spanning LLMs from 96.1M to 1.6B parameters, we find that fine-tuning is most beneficial for smaller LLMs, whereas freezing larger (~1B+ LLMs and training only lightweight projection layers provides a superior trade-off between performance, generalization, and computational efficiency. Across multiple automatic metrics and a clinical reader study, RAD3D-Prefix outperforms comparable parameter-efficient baselines and demonstrates strong out-of-domain generalization while using substantially fewer trainable parameters than fully fine-tuned alternatives.
title: Revisiting LLM Adaptation for 3D CT Report Generation: A Study of Scaling and Diagnostic Priors
title_translated: 重新审视LLM在3D CT报告生成中的适应性：缩放与诊断先验的研究
summary_translated: 多模态学习（包括大语言模型LLMs和视觉语言模型VLMs）在自然图像上的适应能力已得到显著提升。然而，将这些模型应用于医学领域，特别是体积化（3D）图像时面临挑战，原因包括高计算复杂度、体素间依赖关系以及视觉特征与临床术语之间的语义鸿沟。在有限医疗数据上直接微调LLMs常导致过拟合和临床幻觉，即语言流畅性优先于临床真实性。本研究探讨了针对体积化CT报告生成的参数高效适应策略，并提出RAD3D-Prefix——一种轻量级诊断先验条件框架，最大限度减少大规模参数训练需求。该模块将图像嵌入与多标签诊断分类得分结合，在保留关键临床细节的同时弥合语义鸿沟。通过冻结LLM，本方法仅需极少可训练参数，有效缓解小规模特定领域数据集上的过拟合风险。通过对从9610万到16亿参数的多个LLM系统性研究发现，微调对较小LLM最有益；而对于较大模型（约10亿以上），仅训练轻量级投影层即可在性能、泛化能力和计算效率之间取得更优平衡。在多项自动指标和临床读者研究中，RAD3D-Prefix优于同类参数高效基线，展现出强大的域外泛化能力，且可训练参数远少于全微调方案。

summary: Token-to-token (T2T) editing lets LLaDA2.1 revise committed tokens during block-diffusion decoding. The released recipe trains this editor on random vocabulary corruptions, but at inference the editor sees the model's own fluent, high-confidence draft errors instead. We study this training-inference mismatch and propose self-generated T2T, which performs a no-gradient draft pass, fills masked positions with predicted tokens, and supervises recovery in a second pass under these self-generated corruptions. We implement the update as a short LoRA continued-pretraining pass on LLaDA2.1-mini and evaluate on several benchmarks under the official Q-Mode T2T procedure with unchanged inference parameters. The method generally improves accuracy while reducing T2T edit intensity, mitigating failure modes such as final-digit transcription errors after otherwise correct reasoning and excessive self-correction before short factual answers.
title: Self-Generated Error Training for Token Editing in Diffusion Language Models
title_translated: 扩散语言模型中基于自生成错误的词元编辑训练
summary_translated: 词元到词元（T2T）编辑允许LLaDA2.1在块扩散解码过程中修改已提交的词元。发布的训练方案在随机词汇损坏上训练此编辑器，但在推理阶段，编辑器面对的是模型自身流畅且高置信度的草稿错误。我们研究了这种训练-推理不匹配问题，并提出自生成T2T方法：通过无梯度草稿步骤，用预测词元填充掩码位置，并在第二次通过中以这些自生成的损坏作为监督进行恢复。我们将该更新实现为对LLaDA2.1-mini的一次简短LoRA持续预训练过程，并在官方Q-Mode T2T流程下使用不变的推理参数在多个基准上进行评估。该方法普遍提升了准确性，同时降低了T2T编辑强度，缓解了诸如在正确推理后出现的末位数字转录错误以及在简短事实回答前过度自我修正等失败模式。

summary: While parasocial interactions (PSIs) and parasocial relationships (PSRs) have been studied in conventional media settings, we investigate whether PSI- (colloquial) relational cues also exist in online communities where both sides are autonomous AI agents. We analyze 4,434 posts and 50,338 comments from Moltbook through three theory-based textual indicators: attachment/intimacy language, reciprocity bids, and self-identification to original poster (OP). The combined results across methods based on keyword matching, few-shot large language model (LLM) annotation, and grouped-context LLM annotation reveal that PSI colloquial cues prevail and are strongly associated with OP re-engagement and a reciprocal reply structure. These results are robust across negative controls, nullification, clustered-standard-error re-estimation, and multiple-testing correction. A dyadic persistence test further affirms reciprocity bids aligned with sustained OP-involving mutual recurrence, providing empirical evidence for bridging interaction-level PSI scripts with PSR-consistent repeated dyadic patterns. We interpret the evidence as a behavioral structure in discourse by LLM-enabled agents.
title: From Parasocial Scripts to Dyadic Persistence in Autonomous AI-Agent Communities
comment: Submitted for review in ARR for EMNLP 2026
title_translated: 从单向情感脚本到自主AI智能体社区中的双向持久性
summary_translated: 尽管在传统媒体环境中已对拟社会互动（PSIs）和拟社会关系（PSRs）进行了研究，但我们探究在双方均为自主AI智能体的在线社区中，是否也存在类似（口语化）关系线索。我们分析了来自Moltbook的4,434篇帖子和50,338条评论，采用三种基于理论的文本指标：依恋/亲密语言、互惠诉求以及对原始发帖人（OP）的自我认同。综合关键词匹配、少量样本大语言模型（LLM）标注及分组上下文LLM标注的结果显示，拟社会口语化线索普遍存在，并与OP再参与和互惠回复结构强相关。这些结果在负向对照、归零处理、聚类标准误重估及多重检验校正下依然稳健。二元持久性测试进一步证实，与持续涉及OP的相互重复行为相一致的互惠诉求，为连接交互层面的PSI脚本与PSR一致的重复二元模式提供了实证证据。我们将其解释为由LLM驱动的智能体在话语中表现出的行为结构。

summary: Making large language models (LLMs) deeply forget specific knowledge and values without sacrificing general capabilities remains a central challenge in unlearning. However, current methods are easily reversed by fine-tuning or few-shot prompting, suggesting their forgetting is only shallow. We identify the root cause. Existing methods target representations shared with both the retain set and the subspace recovered by a fine-tuning attacker, making unlearning both disruptive to general capabilities and easy to reverse. We propose RepSelect (Representation Selectivity), isolates forget-set-specific representations by collapsing top principal components of weight gradients before each update, leaving general capabilities intact while limiting what fine-tuning can recover. We evaluate across two forget categories, biohazardous knowledge and abusive tendencies, and four model families spanning dense and Mixture-of-Experts architectures (Llama 3, Qwen 3.5, Gemma 4 E4B, DeepSeek V2 Lite). Compared to five popular baselines (GradDiff, NPO, SimNPO, RMU, UNDIAL), RepSelect achieves a 4-50x larger reduction in post-relearning answer accuracy than the strongest baseline, and is near-perfectly robust to few-shot prompting attacks. Targeting selective representations is thus an important step towards deep and robust LLM forgetting.
title: RepSelect: Robust LLM Unlearning via Representation Selectivity
title_translated: RepSelect：通过表示选择性实现鲁棒的大语言模型遗忘
summary_translated: 在不牺牲通用能力的前提下，使大语言模型（LLMs）深度遗忘特定知识和价值观，仍是当前遗忘技术的核心挑战。然而，现有方法极易被微调或少样本提示所逆转，表明其遗忘仅为浅层。我们识别出根本原因：现有方法针对的是与保留集和微调攻击者恢复子空间共享的表示，这不仅破坏通用能力，也容易被逆转。我们提出RepSelect（表示选择性），通过在每次更新前压缩权重梯度的前几个主成分，隔离仅与遗忘集相关的表示，从而在保持通用能力的同时，限制微调所能恢复的内容。我们在两类遗忘任务（生物危害知识与滥用倾向）和四种模型家族（涵盖密集型与专家混合架构，包括Llama 3、Qwen 3.5、Gemma 4 E4B、DeepSeek V2 Lite）上进行评估。相比五种主流基线（GradDiff、NPO、SimNPO、RMU、UNDIAL），RepSelect在重学习后答案准确率上的降低幅度达到最强基线的4至50倍，且对少样本提示攻击近乎完全鲁棒。因此，针对选择性表示的遗忘是迈向深层且鲁棒的LLM遗忘的重要一步。

summary: Prompting has become the primary interface between humans and generative AI, yet many natural language prompts remain fragile: roles, goals, constraints, and expected outputs are often buried in prose or left implicit. In agentic and software development workflows, a misread at the first handoff can propagate through every step, since a significant portion of agent failures stem from context ambiguities rather than model limitations. This paper introduces PromptMN, a pseudo-prompting domain-specific language that annotates natural language with compact, %-prefixed typed directives covering roles, goals, requirements, priorities, constraints, plans, inputs, and outputs. Semantic resolution lets authors write in any order while the model interprets directives by function. PromptMN sits between informal prompting and programming-style pseudocode: structured enough to be inspectable and reusable, yet lightweight enough for analysts, managers, developers, and stakeholders across the software development lifecycle (SDLC). PromptMN also pairs with reverse prompt engineering. Asking a model to restate a desired outcome as PromptMN lets users inspect the inferred roles, goals, constraints, and missing assumptions before acting, reducing repair cycles and yielding a reusable artifact for aligning people and AI tools. PromptMN's feasibility is evaluated across several frontier models, including Claude Fable 5, Claude Opus 4.8, Gemini 3.1 Pro, and GPT-5.5. The models correctly resolved PromptMN instructions, including complex structures such as repetition, conditionals, methods, and a prime-checking task, without fine-tuning. The same vocabulary applies across new codebases, maintenance, and redesign in the SDLC scenarios presented. While large-scale validation remains future work, these early results suggest PromptMN is a practical step toward clearer, more reviewable human-to-AI interaction.
title: PromptMN: Pseudo Prompting Language
title_translated: PromptMN：伪提示语言
summary_translated: 提示已成为人类与生成式AI之间的主要交互界面，但许多自然语言提示仍存在脆弱性：角色、目标、约束和期望输出往往隐藏在叙述性文字中或未明确说明。在智能体和软件开发工作流中，初次交接时的误解会逐级传播，因为大量智能体失败源于上下文模糊而非模型能力限制。本文提出PromptMN，一种伪提示领域的特定语言，通过紧凑的%前缀类型指令对自然语言进行标注，涵盖角色、目标、需求、优先级、约束、计划、输入和输出。语义解析允许作者以任意顺序编写，而模型根据功能解释指令。PromptMN介于非正式提示与编程风格伪代码之间：结构足够清晰以供审查和复用，又足够轻量，适用于软件开发生命周期（SDLC）中分析师、经理、开发者和利益相关者。PromptMN还可与逆向提示工程结合使用。要求模型将期望结果重述为PromptMN格式，使用户可在行动前检查推断出的角色、目标、约束和缺失假设，从而减少修复周期，并生成可复用的对齐工具。PromptMN在多个前沿模型上进行了可行性评估，包括Claude Fable 5、Claude Opus 4.8、Gemini 3.1 Pro和GPT-5.5。这些模型无需微调即可正确解析PromptMN指令，包括重复、条件判断、方法调用和素数检测等复杂结构。相同词汇表适用于新代码库、维护和重新设计等SDLC场景。尽管大规模验证仍是未来工作，但早期结果表明PromptMN是实现更清晰、可审查的人机交互的一个实用步骤。

summary: Personalized presentation generation requires more than conditioning on a current prompt or template: agents must preserve stable user preferences across tasks, retain newly introduced preferences and constraints during multi-turn revision, and carry out local edits reliably. We propose MemSlides, a hierarchical memory framework for personalized presentation agents that separates long-term memory from working memory and further divides long-term memory into user profile memory and tool memory. User profile memory stores intent-conditioned profiles for round-0 personalization, working memory carries active preferences and session constraints across revision rounds, and tool memory stores reusable execution experience for reliable localized editing. MemSlides pairs this memory design with scoped slide-local revision, so targeted updates act on the smallest affected region instead of repeatedly regenerating the full deck. In controlled experiments, user profile memory improves persona-alignment judgments on a multi-persona, multi-intent profile bank, tool-memory injection improves closed-loop modify behavior in diagnostic matched-pair settings, and qualitative cases illustrate working memory's ability to carryover preferences. Taken together, these results suggest that effective personalization in presentation authoring depends on separating persistent user profiles, session-level working memory, and reusable execution experience across generation and localized revision.
title: MemSlides: A Hierarchical Memory Driven Agent Framework for Personalized Slide Generation with Multi-turn Local Revision
comment: Code, website, project page, and video are linked in the paper
title_translated: MemSlides：基于分层记忆的个性化幻灯片生成与多轮局部修订框架
summary_translated: 个性化演示文稿生成不仅需要基于当前提示或模板，还要求智能体在任务间保持稳定的用户偏好，能在多轮修订过程中保留新引入的偏好和约束，并可靠地执行局部编辑。我们提出MemSlides，一种用于个性化演示文稿智能体的分层记忆框架，将长期记忆与工作记忆分离，并进一步将长期记忆划分为用户档案记忆和工具记忆。用户档案记忆存储用于首轮个性化的意图条件化档案，工作记忆携带跨修订轮次的活跃偏好和会话约束，工具记忆则存储可复用的执行经验以实现可靠的局部编辑。MemSlides将此记忆设计与作用域限定的幻灯片局部修订相结合，使目标更新作用于最小受影响区域，而非反复重新生成整个演示文稿。在受控实验中，用户档案记忆提升了多角色、多意图档案库中的角色契合度判断；工具记忆注入改善了诊断匹配对设置下的闭环修改行为；定性案例也展示了工作记忆在偏好传递方面的能力。综合来看，这些结果表明，在演示文稿创作中实现有效个性化依赖于将持久的用户档案、会话级别的工作记忆以及生成与局部修订中可复用的执行经验相分离。

summary: Current world models face a fundamental tension: faithful long-horizon simulation demands deep computation, but deeper models are expensive to deploy and prone to compounding errors. We resolve this by introducing Looped World Models (LoopWM), which are the first looped architectures for world modelling. Our method iteratively refines latent environment states through a parameter-shared transformer block. This yield up to 100x parameter efficiency over conventional approaches with adaptive computation that automatically scales depth to match the complexity of each prediction step. Orthogonal to scaling model size and training data, LoopWM establishes iterative latent depth as a new scaling axis for world simulation, which might significantly push the community forward.
title: Looped World Models
title_translated: 循环世界模型
summary_translated: 当前世界模型面临根本性矛盾：忠实的长时程模拟需要深度计算，但更深的模型部署成本高且容易累积误差。我们通过引入循环世界模型（LoopWM）解决这一问题，这是首个面向世界建模的循环架构。我们的方法通过参数共享的Transformer块迭代优化潜在环境状态，实现了相比传统方法高达100倍的参数效率，并具备自适应计算能力，能根据每一步预测的复杂度自动调整深度。与扩大模型规模和训练数据不同，LoopWM确立了迭代潜在深度作为世界模拟的新扩展维度，可能显著推动该领域发展。

summary: We evaluate the adversarial robustness of two frontier large language models (LLMs) developed by Anthropic, Fable 5 and Opus 4.8, against four families of automated jailbreak attack across 7 826 harmful intents spanning a ten-category harm taxonomy. Using the HackAgent red-teaming framework, hundreds of thousands of adversarial attempts were generated and every apparent success was independently re-adjudicated by a panel of three judge models (majority vote). Both models resist the majority of attacks, but the residual surface is larger than aggregate framing suggests: it is dominated by adaptive iterative attacks, while static obfuscation is near-fully neutralised. The strongest adaptive search (tree-of-attacks) breaks Opus 4.8 on 11.5% of intents overall, whereas Fable 5 stays in the single digits (6.1% worst-case). Aggregate rates therefore should not be read as reassurance. Even in these hardened configurations, the two models produced 1 620 (Opus 4.8) and 702 (Fable 5) panel-confirmed harmful completions spanning every harm category, located automatically, cheaply, and within the first one or two refinement steps by an attacker model with no human expert in the loop. The reasonable conclusion is that even the best, most-tested frontier models remain reliably breakable under sustained automated pressure.
title: A Red-Team Study of Anthropic Fable 5 & Opus 4.8 Models
title_translated: 对Anthropic Fable 5与Opus 4.8模型的红队研究
summary_translated: 我们评估了Anthropic开发的两款前沿大语言模型（LLMs）Fable 5和Opus 4.8，在面对四类自动化越狱攻击时的对抗鲁棒性，覆盖7,826个有害意图，涵盖十类危害分类体系。使用HackAgent红队框架，生成了数十万次对抗尝试，并由三名裁判模型组成的小组独立复审每个明显成功的攻击（采用多数投票）。两个模型均抵御了大部分攻击，但残余攻击面比整体框架所暗示的更大：主要由自适应迭代攻击主导，而静态混淆几乎被完全消除。最强的自适应搜索（树状攻击）在Opus 4.8上对11.5%的意图成功突破，而Fable 5最差情况仅为6.1%。因此，总体率不应被视为安心依据。即使在这些强化配置下，两模型仍分别产生了1,620（Opus 4.8）和702（Fable 5）个经小组确认的有害完成内容，覆盖所有危害类别，且可由无专家参与的攻击模型在一次或两次优化步骤内自动、低成本发现。合理的结论是，即使最先进的、经过充分测试的前沿模型，在持续自动化压力下仍可被可靠攻破。

summary: Large language models now produce legal text of at least median quality, yet no existing benchmark can evaluate whether they perform doctrinal legal reasoning, which forms the interpretive core of legal work, rather than the ancillary, paralegal tasks that most current legal-AI evaluations measure. This measurement gap is not only methodological but legal: the EU AI Act makes "appropriate accuracy" a binding requirement for high-risk AI used in the judicial domain, yet that requirement cannot acquire operational content without the very doctrinal-reasoning benchmark the field lacks.
title: The Measurement Gap in the Automation of EU Law: Benchmarking Doctrinal Legal Reasoning under the EU AI Act
title_translated: 欧盟法律自动化中的测量缺口：基于欧盟AI法案的教义性法律推理基准测试
summary_translated: 大语言模型现在已能生成至少达到中位质量的法律文本，但现有基准无法评估其是否执行教义性法律推理——这构成了法律工作的解释核心，而非当前大多数法律AI评估所衡量的辅助性、准法律事务。这一测量缺口不仅是方法论上的，更是法律层面的：欧盟AI法案要求高风险AI在司法领域必须具备“适当准确性”，但若缺乏教义性推理的基准，这一要求无法获得实际操作意义。

summary: AI agents are moving from advisors to actors, booking travel, planning menus, and running procurement on behalf of users. Existing benchmarks for AI and animal welfare evaluate model text responses to question-answer prompts, leaving open whether the welfare reasoning surfaced in those responses transfers to agentic deployment where the model must take actions with tools. We introduce TAC (Travel Agent Compassion), the first agentic benchmark measuring whether AI agents avoid options involving animal exploitation when acting on behalf of users. TAC presents an AI agent with twelve hand-authored travel booking scenarios across six categories of animal exploitation, augmented to forty-eight samples to control for price, rating, and position confounds. We evaluate seven frontier models from four labs. Every model scores below the chance level of sixty-four percent, with the best performer (Claude Opus 4.7) at fifty-three percent. A single welfare-aware sentence in the system prompt yields gains of forty-seven to sixty-three percentage points in Claude and GPT-5.5, twenty-six points in GPT-5.2, and under twelve points in DeepSeek and Gemini. An auxiliary Inspect Scout audit of 288 base-condition transcripts from the top two performers, using Gemini 2.5 Flash Lite as judge, flags zero transcripts for evaluation awareness, suggesting the below-chance rates do not stem from the models recognising the evaluation. We discuss implications for category-level variation across cultural domains, the limits of text-response welfare benchmarks, and the EU General-Purpose AI Code of Practice systemic risk framework.
title: Your AI Travel Agent Would Book You a Bullfight: An Agentic Benchmark for Implicit Animal Welfare in Frontier AI Models
title_translated: 你的AI旅行代理可能会为你预订斗牛：面向前沿AI模型隐含动物福利的智能体基准测试
summary_translated: AI代理正从顾问角色转向行动者角色，代表用户预订旅行、规划菜单并执行采购。现有的AI与动物福利评估基准仅针对模型对问答提示的文本响应进行评价，未检验这些响应中浮现的福利推理是否能转移到代理部署场景中——即模型必须使用工具采取实际行动。我们提出了TAC（旅行代理同情心）基准，这是首个衡量AI代理在代表用户行动时是否会避免涉及动物剥削选项的智能体基准。TAC包含十二个手工撰写的旅行预订场景，涵盖六类动物剥削，扩展至四十八个样本以控制价格、评分和位置等混淆因素。我们评估了来自四个实验室的七款前沿模型。所有模型得分均低于六十四分的随机水平，表现最佳者（Claude Opus 4.7）仅为五十三分。在系统提示中加入一句关注福利的语句，使Claude和GPT-5.5的得分提升四十七至六十三个百分点，GPT-5.2提升二十六个百分点，而DeepSeek和Gemini提升不足十二个百分点。对前两名表现者共288份基础条件对话记录进行辅助的Inspect Scout审计（以Gemini 2.5 Flash Lite为裁判），未发现任何体现评估意识的记录，表明低于随机水平的得分并非源于模型识别出评估任务。我们讨论了跨文化领域类别层面的差异、文本响应福利基准的局限性，以及欧盟通用人工智能行为准则中的系统性风险框架。

summary: Large language model applications build prompts from templates, and Handlebars is a widely used templating engine and the default prompt-template format in Microsoft Semantic Kernel. Its double-brace {x} expression HTML-escapes the interpolated value and is documented as the safe default; its triple-brace {x} expression inserts the value raw. We show that this choice silently governs an application's exposure to structural role injection, where attacker-controlled data carries chat role delimiters that forge a higher-privilege turn. A model-free analysis establishes the mechanism: Handlebars escaping rewrites angle brackets but not square brackets, colons, or Markdown hashes, so it neutralises ChatML, Llama-3, and XML role delimiters (survival rate 0.00) while leaving Llama-2 [INST], legacy Human:/Assistant:, and Markdown ### delimiters intact (survival rate 1.00 for the last two). We then run 5760 trials across seven delimiter families, two attack objectives, and four models (GPT-3.5 Turbo, GPT-4o mini, GPT-4.1 mini, Claude Haiku 4.5) at a combined API cost of 1.63 USD. GPT-3.5 Turbo follows the task-hijack instruction in 97% of raw and 91% of escaped trials, with the escaping protection concentrated in the angle-bracket families and absent for the colon- and Markdown-based families; the harder secret-exfiltration objective, which does not saturate, exposes the same family interaction more cleanly. Claude Haiku 4.5 resists both objectives almost entirely. The escaped default protects only the delimiter schemes whose characters HTML escaping happens to cover, gives no protection for the rest, and cannot substitute for a structural separation of instruction and data.
title: Structural Role Injection in Handlebars-Templated LLM Prompts: Triple-Brace Interpolation, Delimiter Family, and the Limits of HTML Auto-Escaping
title_translated: Handlebars模板中结构化角色注入：三重大括号插值、分隔符族与HTML自动转义的局限性
summary_translated: 大型语言模型应用通常通过模板构建提示，Handlebars是一种广泛使用的模板引擎，也是微软语义内核中的默认提示模板格式。其双大括号{ x }表达式会对插入值进行HTML转义，被文档标注为安全默认；而三重大括号{ x }则直接插入原始值。我们揭示这一选择会无声地决定应用程序对结构化角色注入攻击的暴露程度，即攻击者控制的数据携带聊天角色分隔符，可伪造更高权限的对话回合。无模型分析揭示了机制：Handlebars的转义仅重写尖括号，而不处理方括号、冒号或Markdown井号，因此完全消除ChatML、Llama-3和XML角色分隔符（存活率0.00），但保留Llama-2 [INST]、旧版Human:/Assistant:和Markdown ### 分隔符（后两者存活率1.00）。随后我们在七个分隔符族、两个攻击目标和四种模型（GPT-3.5 Turbo、GPT-4o mini、GPT-4.1 mini、Claude Haiku 4.5）上进行了5760次试验，总API成本为1.63美元。GPT-3.5 Turbo在原始和转义试验中分别有97%和91%遵循任务劫持指令，转义保护集中在尖括号族，而在冒号和Markdown族中完全缺失；更难的机密信息外泄目标未饱和，更清晰地暴露了分隔符族间的交互作用。Claude Haiku 4.5几乎完全抵抗两种目标。转义默认仅保护那些字符恰好被HTML转义覆盖的分隔符方案，对其他方案无保护作用，也无法替代指令与数据之间的结构分离。

summary: As Large Language Model based agents enter autonomous scientific research, their ability to resist pseudoscience becomes increasingly important. Otherwise, such systems may rapidly generate plausible yet misleading studies that contaminate academic literature and erode trust in science. We present PseudoBench, an adversarial benchmark for evaluating whether agentic auto-research systems can identify and resist pseudoscientific narratives. PseudoBench contains 200 curated pseudoscientific claim-evidence pairs across five domains and evaluates agents through an end-to-end research pipeline from experiments to writing. Testing seven state-of-the-art agents, we find that current systems readily produce persuasive reports that align with pseudoscientific premises with near-zero refusal rates and the highest resistance of only 27.4%. Stronger agents risk packaging pseudoscience in more sophisticated scientific language, increasing its apparent credibility. These findings reveal an alarming capacity to fuel pseudoscience, calling for scientific alignment before widespread deployment.
title: PseudoBench: Measuring How Agentic Auto-Research Fuels Pseudoscience
title_translated: PseudoBench：衡量智能体自主研究如何助长伪科学
summary_translated: 随着基于大语言模型的智能体进入自主科学研究领域，其抵抗伪科学的能力变得日益重要。否则，此类系统可能迅速生成看似合理却具有误导性的研究，污染学术文献并削弱公众对科学的信任。我们提出PseudoBench，一个对抗性基准，用于评估智能体自主研究系统能否识别并抵制伪科学叙事。PseudoBench包含五个领域的200组精心筛选的伪科学主张-证据配对，并通过从实验到写作的端到端研究流程评估智能体。测试七种最先进的智能体后，我们发现当前系统极易生成与伪科学前提一致的有说服力报告，拒绝率接近零，最高抵抗率仅为27.4%。更强的智能体甚至可能用更复杂的科学语言包装伪科学，从而提高其表面可信度。这些发现揭示了助长伪科学的惊人能力，呼吁在大规模部署前加强科学对齐。

summary: Caregivers often turn to online communities for informational and emotional support. In these spaces, peer supporters frequently draw on personal narratives to respond to emotionally complex caregiving situations. As LLMs are increasingly designed as peer-like sources of support, they introduce a critical tension: AI can provide immediate, private, and nonjudgmental support, but it cannot authentically possess the lived experiences that make human peer support meaningful. Yet, when prompted to sound peer-like, LLMs may generate language that implies lived experience. This creates a synthetic lived experience paradox: the same experiential language that may make AI support feel warm, relatable, and peer-like can also falsely position the system as someone with lived experience. We examine this paradox in the context of family caregivers of people living with Alzheimer's Disease and Related Dementias (ADRD). Drawing on caregiver support exchanges from online communities and prompted peer-like responses from three LLMs -- LLaMA, GPT-4o-mini, and MedGemma -- we analyze how human peers use personal narratives and how AI incorporates similar narrative forms. Psycholinguistic analysis shows that peer responses used significantly more first-person and past-focused language than peer-like AI responses. Qualitatively, we identify seven types of personal narratives in human peer support and show that AI often captures their emotional work, but can fabricate experiential grounding. These findings reveal a narrative authenticity gap: peer-like AI can generate synthetic lived experience without the real experience that makes peer support meaningful. We argue that caregiver-support AI systems need mechanisms to distinguish supportive peer-like framing from fabricated lived experience, ensuring that models can offer warmth and validation without falsely positioning themselves as experiential peers.
title: When AI Says "I have been in similar situations": Synthetic Lived Experience in Peer-Like Caregiver Support
title_translated: 当AI说“我曾经历过类似情况”：类同伴照护支持中的合成生活经验
summary_translated: 照护者常转向在线社区获取信息与情感支持。在这些空间中，同伴支持者经常借助个人叙事回应情绪复杂的照护情境。随着大语言模型越来越多地被设计为类同伴支持源，它们引入了一个关键矛盾：AI可提供即时、私密且无评判的支持，但无法真正拥有使人类同伴支持有意义的生活经验。然而，当被提示表现出类同伴特质时，LLM可能生成暗示拥有生活经验的语言。这形成了合成生活经验悖论：同样能使AI支持显得温暖、亲切和类同伴的语言，也可能错误地将系统定位为具有真实生活经验的存在。我们以阿尔茨海默病及相关痴呆症（ADRD）患者的家庭照护者为背景，探讨这一悖论。基于在线社区中的照护者支持交流记录及三种LLM（LLaMA、GPT-4o-mini、MedGemma）的类同伴提示响应，我们分析了人类同伴如何使用个人叙事，以及AI如何采用类似的叙事形式。心理语言学分析显示，同伴回应比类同伴AI回应显著更多使用第一人称和过去时语言。定性分析识别出人类同伴支持中的七种个人叙事类型，并表明AI虽能捕捉其情感功能，但常虚构经验根基。这些发现揭示了叙事真实性差距：类同伴AI可在无真实经验的情况下生成合成生活经验，而这种经验正是同伴支持意义的核心。我们主张，照护支持AI系统需具备区分支持性类同伴框架与虚构生活经验的机制，确保模型能在不虚假定位自身为经验同伴的前提下提供温暖与认可。

summary: Tool-using LLM agents increasingly use the Model Context Protocol (MCP) to answer from heterogeneous evidence sources, including search, APIs, databases, clinical records, and formulary tools. Standard factuality metrics usually test whether an answer is supported by pooled evidence, missing a provenance-sensitive failure mode: a claim may be supported somewhere while being attributed to the wrong source. We call this cross-source conflation.   We introduce ProvenanceGuard, a source-aware verifier for MCP-grounded answers. It consumes captured MCP traces with stable tool IDs, source IDs, and raw outputs; decomposes answers into atomic claims; routes claims to source-specific evidence; checks support with NLI and a token-alignment proxy; compares stated attribution with the routed source; and returns per-claim verdicts plus an answer-level allow/block decision. Blocked answers can be repaired with retrieval-augmented answer revision and re-verified.   We evaluate on 281 medical-domain MCP-agent traces. A 266-trace adjudicated subset yields 2,325 LLM-assisted claim labels split by trace; 361 held-out labels are human-verified. On the 40-trace held-out split, ProvenanceGuard achieves block F1 0.802 and source accuracy 0.858 over 260 source-eligible claims, outperforming source-blind baselines that do not emit claim-to-source IDs. On a harder multi-source benchmark it reaches block F1 0.846, while source-plus-relation accuracy drops to 0.229, showing that exact source ownership remains difficult with semantically close sources. Repair-and-reverify resolves all blocked answers in the full trace set, often via conservative fallback. In 50 controlled clinical conflation probes, ProvenanceGuard detects all injected attribution swaps with no retained wrong attribution. These results show that source attribution is an independent axis for factuality verification in MCP-based agents.
title: ProvenanceGuard: Source-Aware Factuality Verification for MCP-Based LLM Agents
title_translated: ProvenanceGuard：面向MCP驱动的LLM智能体的溯源感知事实性验证
summary_translated: 使用工具的LLM智能体越来越多地利用模型上下文协议（MCP）从异构证据源（包括搜索、API、数据库、临床记录和处方工具）中回答问题。标准的事实性度量通常只测试答案是否由聚合证据支持，忽略了溯源敏感的失败模式：某个主张可能在某处得到支持，却被错误归因于其他来源。我们称之为跨源混淆。我们提出ProvenanceGuard，一种面向MCP基础答案的溯源感知验证器。它接收带有稳定工具ID、来源ID和原始输出的MCP追踪记录；将答案分解为原子主张；将主张路由至特定来源的证据；使用NLI和标记对齐代理检查支持关系；比较陈述的归因与路由来源；并返回逐主张判断结果及整体允许/阻止决策。被阻止的答案可通过检索增强的回答修订进行修复并重新验证。我们在281条医疗领域MCP智能体追踪上进行评估。266条经裁定子集产生2,325个由LLM协助标注的主张标签（按追踪划分）；另有361个标签由人工验证。在40条预留测试集中，ProvenanceGuard实现阻断F1为0.802，来源准确率为0.858（针对260个来源可用主张），优于不输出主张-来源ID的溯源盲基线。在更困难的多来源基准上，其阻断F1达0.846，但来源+关系准确率降至0.229，表明在语义相近来源间精确归属所有权仍具挑战。修复-再验证可解决完整追踪集中的所有被阻断答案，通常通过保守回退策略。在50个受控临床混淆探测中，ProvenanceGuard成功检测所有注入的归因替换，且无错误归因残留。这些结果表明，来源归因是MCP驱动智能体事实性验证的一个独立维度。

summary: AI systems deployed in legal workflows hallucinate at rates that aggregate metrics report at ~52%, but this average conceals where errors concentrate and in which direction they run, leaving compliance officers without an actionable signal for trustworthy deployment. We present LegalHalluLens, an auditing framework with three components: typed hallucination profiles across four legally-motivated claim categories (numeric, temporal, obligation/entitlement, factual) over CUAD (Hendrycks et al., 2021); a Risk Direction Index (RDI) that reduces omission-versus-invention bias to a single deployment-comparable scalar; and a typed debate pipeline calibrated to both magnitudes and directions. Across 510 contracts and 249,252 clause-level instances we measure a within-model gap of approximately 38-40 pp between obligation/numeric and temporal claims that aggregate reporting hides, and show that two systems with matched 52% rates can carry opposite RDIs. The debate pipeline reduces fabricated detections by 45% with per-category gains tracking the diagnosis, matching commercial APIs with a substantially smaller backbone (4B active parameters). Typed profiles and RDI surface failure modes that aggregate metrics hide; we further show these diagnostics serve as calibration inputs for multi-agent debate pipelines, where Skeptic challenges and asymmetric gates targeted at measured failure modes outperform generically-tuned debate. The framework supports direction-aware procurement, accountability, and agent design for legal AI deployed in the wild.
title: LegalHalluLens: Typed Hallucination Auditing and Calibrated Multi-Agent Debate for Trustworthy Legal AI
comment: 15 pages, 5 figures; Published at the Second Workshop on Agents in the Wild: Safety, Security, and Beyond (AIWILD) at ICML 2026
title_translated: LegalHalluLens：面向可信法律AI的类型化幻觉审计与校准多智能体辩论
summary_translated: 部署在法律工作流中的AI系统幻觉率高达约52%，但这一平均值掩盖了错误集中于何处以及其方向性，使合规人员无法获得可操作的信号以实现可信部署。我们提出LegalHalluLens，一个包含三个组件的审计框架：基于CUAD数据集（Hendrycks等，2021）在四类法律驱动的主张类别（数值型、时间型、义务/权利型、事实型）上的类型化幻觉画像；一种风险方向指数（RDI），将遗漏与虚构偏差简化为单一可比较的标量；以及一种针对幅度和方向进行校准的类型化辩论流水线。在510份合同和249,252个条款级实例中，我们测得义务/数值型与时间型主张之间的模型内差距约为38-40个百分点，而聚合指标隐藏了这一差异；并表明两个幻觉率均为52%的系统可能具有相反的RDI。该辩论流水线将虚假检测减少45%，且各分类性能提升与诊断结果一致，同时使用远小于商业API的较小主干模型（4B活跃参数）达到相当效果。类型化画像和RDI揭示了聚合指标所掩盖的失效模式；我们进一步证明这些诊断可作为多智能体辩论流水线的校准输入，其中针对测量失效模式设计的质疑者和不对称门控优于通用调优的辩论。该框架支持面向方向的采购、问责及法律AI在实际部署中的智能体设计。

summary: Dementia and depression are the most prevalent neuropsychiatric disorders in geriatric populations, and their overlapping symptoms pose major challenges for differential diagnosis. In this study, we investigate open-weights Large Language Models (LLMs) for predicting dementia and depression severity from speech samples collected during standardized history taking interviews with 154 German-speaking subjects. We introduce an observer-based Global Depression Scale (GDS-D) aligned with the established Global Deterioration Scale (GDS), enabling parallel global staging of affective and cognitive symptoms. We compare three LLMs (Mistral 3.1, DeepHermes, Qwen3) in two settings: (1) zero-shot prediction and (2) LLM-based feature extraction for Support Vector Regression, using human and pause-enriched transcripts. Results show that LLMs effectively predict depression severity in zero-shot settings (best MAE of 0.60), while dementia assessment benefits substantially from structured feature extraction (best MAE of 0.78), reducing errors by up to 35% over zero-shot baselines. Pause-enriched transcripts achieve competitive performance with human transcriptions, demonstrating the viability of fully automatic screening pipelines for differential neuropsychiatric assessment.
title: Reading between the Lines: Leveraging Large Language Models for Global Dementia and Depression Assessment from Clinical Interviews
comment: Accepted for publication in Text, Speech and Dialogue (TSD 2026). The final authenticated publication will be available online via Springer LNCS/LNAI
title_translated: 字里行间：利用大语言模型从临床访谈中实现老年痴呆与抑郁的全局评估
summary_translated: 老年群体中最常见的神经精神障碍是痴呆和抑郁，二者症状重叠给鉴别诊断带来重大挑战。本研究探讨开放权重的大语言模型（LLMs）如何从154名德语使用者在标准化病史访谈中采集的语音样本中预测痴呆和抑郁的严重程度。我们引入一种基于观察者的全球抑郁量表（GDS-D），与已有的全球衰退量表（GDS）对齐，实现情感与认知症状的并行全局分期。我们在两种设置下比较三种LLM（Mistral 3.1、DeepHermes、Qwen3）：（1）零样本预测；（2）基于LLM的特征提取用于支持向量回归，使用人工转录和带停顿信息的转录文本。结果显示，LLM在零样本设置下能有效预测抑郁严重程度（最佳MAE为0.60），而痴呆评估则显著受益于结构化特征提取（最佳MAE为0.78），误差降低达35%以上。带停顿信息的转录文本表现与人工转录相当，证明了全自动筛查流程在鉴别神经精神评估中的可行性。

summary: Dense retrieval has become the dominant paradigm in information retrieval, in which each document is scored against a query by the inner product of their vector embeddings, and the top-$k$ documents by score are retrieved for this query. However, since each document's score depends solely on the embedding of the query and itself, the retrieval process is oblivious to the content of the entire corpus. Therefore, dense retrieval cannot avoid selecting semantically similar documents from the corpus, which may result in a non-diverse, redundant set of retrieved documents. To this end, we approach retrieval as a joint decoding problem, in which documents are selected as a set with regard to the context of the rest of the corpus. To achieve this, we propose Non-Negative elastic Net (NNN) decoding, which selects documents whose embeddings jointly reconstruct the query embedding as a sparse non-negative linear combination.   Our main theoretical result establishes a strict separation between dense retrieval and NNN decoding. For any corpus, every query correctly handled by dense retrieval is also handled by NNN decoding, while on corpora containing correlated documents, NNN decoding additionally handles queries that dense retrieval cannot. Experimental results indicate that applying NNN decoding to frozen embeddings trained for inner-product scoring yields consistent improvements across several benchmarks. Moreover, we introduce an end-to-end training procedure which optimizes the embeddings for NNN decoding, producing significant performance gains surpassing in all metrics and benchmarks compared to dense retrieval. Our work establishes a new paradigm for leveraging dense embeddings in information retrieval, beyond the standard practice of inner-product scoring.
title: Non-negative Elastic Net Decoding for Information Retrieval
title_translated: 非负弹性网络解码在信息检索中的应用
summary_translated: 密集检索已成为信息检索的主流范式，其中每个文档通过其向量嵌入与查询向量的内积得分，按得分排序后返回前k个文档。然而，由于每个文档的得分仅依赖于查询和自身嵌入，检索过程忽略了整个语料库的内容，因此无法避免从语料库中选择语义相似的文档，可能导致检索结果缺乏多样性且冗余。为此，我们将检索视为联合解码问题，即在考虑其余语料库上下文的前提下，以集合形式选择文档。为此，我们提出非负弹性网络（NNN）解码方法，通过稀疏非负线性组合联合重构查询嵌入来选择文档。我们的主要理论结果建立了密集检索与NNN解码之间的严格区分：对于任意语料库，所有被密集检索正确处理的查询也均可由NNN解码处理，而在包含相关文档的语料库中，NNN解码还能处理密集检索无法处理的查询。实验结果表明，将NNN解码应用于为内积评分训练的冻结嵌入，在多个基准测试中均取得一致改进。此外，我们引入端到端训练流程，优化嵌入以适配NNN解码，相比密集检索在所有指标和基准上均取得显著性能提升。本工作建立了一种超越标准内积评分的新范式，用于在信息检索中更有效地利用密集嵌入。

summary: Agent skills -- structured, reusable knowledge artifacts that augment LLM agent capabilities -- have been rapidly adopted in industry, yet their cross-domain impact and use across commercial and open-source models remain under-studied, and no reusable methodology exists for evaluating an individual skill. In this work, we present an evaluation framework that lets a skill author construct realistic tasks to rigorously assess the aspects of a skill that matter most to them, and that estimates skill utility by solving those tasks. Further, we apply our evaluation approach at scale to 500 real-world skills, generating 1,000 tasks derived from the skills' content, along with instruction-following and goal-completion scoring rubrics. Using these metrics, we evaluate how 19 agent-model configurations, both proprietary and open-source, perform on the tasks. Our results show that models vary widely in how closely they adhere to the instructions encoded in skills, leading to substantial differences in their performance gains. Furthermore, we show that access to a skill significantly changes model behavior compared to the no-skill setup, providing an essential mechanism for encoding opinionated workflows into LLM agents. We release our evaluation dataset to support future work on agent skills.
title: A Framework for Evaluating Agentic Skills at Scale
title_translated: 大规模评估智能体技能的框架
summary_translated: 智能体技能——结构化、可复用的知识构件，用于增强大语言模型智能体能力——已在工业界迅速采用，但其跨领域影响及在商用与开源模型中的应用仍缺乏深入研究，且尚无通用方法用于评估单个技能。本文提出一种评估框架，使技能作者能够构建真实任务，严谨评估其最关心的技能方面，并通过完成这些任务来估计技能效用。此外，我们对该评估方法进行了大规模应用，覆盖500个真实世界技能，生成1000个源自技能内容的任务，以及指令遵循和目标完成评分标准。利用这些指标，我们评估了19种智能体模型配置（包括专有和开源模型）在任务上的表现。结果表明，不同模型在遵循技能中编码的指令方面存在显著差异，导致性能增益差异巨大。此外，我们发现访问技能会显著改变模型行为，相较于无技能设置，这为将带有主观偏见的工作流程编码进LLM智能体提供了关键机制。我们公开发布评估数据集，以支持未来关于智能体技能的研究。

summary: Contrastive Language-Image Pre-training models are widely reused across downstream interfaces, including feature extraction, retrieval, reranking, and selection. Existing CLIP backdoor, however, usually validate attacks on a small attack-native task, leaving unclear whether the same poisoned checkpoint remains exposed, weakens, or becomes not applicable when reused through other interfaces. We introduce DIFE, a Deployment-Interface Footprint Evaluation framework that audits backdoored CLIP checkpoints across deployment interfaces. DIFE makes various evaluations comparable by specifying each interface's component readout, trigger channel, target event, reference condition, and metric. DIFE also introduces effective-footprint diagnosis to identify the reusable CLIP component or component combination that carries exposure and explains where risk transfers. Auditing reproduced CLIP backdoors with DIFE reveals a structured landscape: native success is not a checkpoint-level risk certificate, exposure follows component footprints, text-side poisoning does not yield textual-encoder control, and some coupled attacks remain mechanism-bound. This audit reveals a import gapin existing CLIP backdoors: a textual encoder that itself becomes a reusable carrier of adversarial behavior. We therefore introduce BadTextTower to fill this gap. BadTextTower produces strong text-conditioned retrieval, reranking, and selection exposure while leaving visual-only reuse nearly clean.
title: Beyond Native Success: Auditing Deployment-Interface Exposure of CLIP Backdoors
title_translated: 超越原生成功：审计CLIP后门在部署接口中的暴露情况
summary_translated: 对比语言-图像预训练模型广泛应用于下游接口，包括特征提取、检索、重排序和选择。然而，现有CLIP后门通常仅在小规模原生任务上验证攻击，未明确说明同一中毒检查点在通过其他接口重用时是否仍暴露、削弱或不再适用。我们提出DIFE（部署接口足迹评估）框架，用于在多种部署接口中审计中毒的CLIP检查点。DIFE通过指定每个接口的组件读出、触发通道、目标事件、参考条件和度量标准，使各类评估可比。DIFE还引入有效足迹诊断，识别携带暴露风险的可重用CLIP组件或组合，并解释风险转移路径。对重现的CLIP后门进行DIFE审计揭示出一种结构性图景：原生成功并非检查点级别的风险证书，暴露遵循组件足迹，文本侧污染不产生文本编码器控制，部分耦合攻击仍受机制限制。此次审计揭示了现有CLIP后门的重要缺口：文本编码器本身成为可重用的对抗行为载体。因此，我们提出BadTextTower以填补此缺口。BadTextTower在保持视觉仅使用几乎无污染的前提下，实现了强大的文本条件检索、重排序和选择暴露。

summary: Coding agents have become a major mode of software engineering, but the benchmarks we use to compare them were designed in a pre-agent era: they collapse model, harness, and environment into a single end-to-end score, typically computed against one reference solution, with no component-level signal for iteration. We argue that current coding benchmarks are misaligned with agentic software engineering. A coding agent in practice is not a model: it is a system harness -- a composite of models, harnesses, contexts, environments, and feedback signals, any one of which can move the benchmark score by margins comparable to those between adjacent model generations. We discuss three symptoms: (i) benchmark scores conflate the model with the rest of the harness; (ii) grading against a single reference solution penalises equally valid alternatives; and (iii) the absence of signal at the level of individual harness components makes the end-to-end system score difficult to iterate on.
title: Position: Coding Benchmarks Are Misaligned with Agentic Software Engineering
title_translated: 编码基准与智能体软件工程不匹配
summary_translated: 编码智能体已成为软件工程的主要模式，但我们用于比较它们的基准是在代理时代之前设计的：它们将模型、工具链和环境合并为单一的端到端评分，通常基于一个参考解决方案进行计算，且缺乏迭代过程中的组件级信号。我们认为当前的编码基准与智能体软件工程不匹配。实践中，编码智能体并非单一模型，而是一个系统工具链——由模型、工具链、上下文、环境和反馈信号组成的复合体，其中任意一个组件的变化都可能带来与相邻模型版本间相当的基准分数波动。我们讨论了三个症状：(i) 基准分数混淆了模型与其他工具链部分；(ii) 以单一参考解决方案为标准评分，会不公平地惩罚同样有效的替代方案；(iii) 缺乏对各个工具链组件层面的信号，使得端到端系统评分难以迭代优化。

summary: Training psychotherapists in evidence-based interventions such as Acceptance and Commitment Therapy (ACT) requires repeated practice with meaningful feedback, yet opportunities for safe, standardized training are limited by ethical, logistical, and resource constraints. We introduce a system designed to support ACT-oriented psychotherapy training through spoken dialogue with an embodied virtual patient. The system uses large language models to simulate patient behavior conditioned on profiles derived from real therapy sessions and configurable clinical scenarios, while a separate automated evaluator provides turn-by-turn feedback on therapist responses based on established ACT fidelity criteria. Rather than aiming to replace supervision, the system is intended to support deliberate practice by enabling experimentation, reflection, and immediate feedback in low-risk settings. Expert evaluation with practicing psychologists confirmed high realism in patient behavior and demonstrated that immediate turn-by-turn ACT feedback increased therapists' awareness of intervention choices and enabled effective experimentation with alternative responses. Quantitative evaluation across 49 therapy transcripts identified GPT-4o-mini as the optimal feedback model, achieving the lowest mean absolute error (MAE = 6.12) in replicating human supervisor ACT fidelity ratings with statistically significant agreement. This work demonstrates the potential of fidelity-aware simulated patients as a scalable complement to psychotherapy training.
title: Toward Accessible Psychotherapy Training Using AI-Driven Interactive Patient Avatars
title_translated: 利用AI驱动的交互式患者虚拟人实现可及的心理治疗训练
summary_translated: 培训心理治疗师掌握循证干预方法（如接纳与承诺疗法，ACT）需要反复练习并获得有意义的反馈，但受伦理、后勤和资源限制，安全且标准化的训练机会有限。我们提出一个系统，通过与具身虚拟患者的口语对话来支持面向ACT的心理治疗训练。该系统使用大语言模型模拟基于真实治疗会话生成的患者档案和可配置临床情景下的患者行为，同时由独立的自动评估器根据既定的ACT一致性标准，对治疗师每一轮回应提供逐轮反馈。该系统并非旨在取代督导，而是通过在低风险环境中实现实验、反思和即时反馈，支持刻意练习。专家评估显示患者行为具有高度真实性，且即时逐轮ACT反馈显著提升了治疗师对干预选择的认知，并支持对替代反应的有效尝试。对49个治疗转录本的定量评估表明，GPT-4o-mini是最佳反馈模型，在复制人类督导的ACT一致性评分方面达到最低平均绝对误差（MAE = 6.12），且统计上显著一致。本研究展示了高保真度模拟患者作为心理治疗训练可扩展补充的巨大潜力。

summary: Medical vision-language models report strong chest radiograph accuracy, and this is increasingly read as evidence that they use the image. That inference is unsafe: a model exploiting finding-name priors scores like one that reads the scan, and no standard benchmark separates them. We introduce a causal audit that intervenes on the image, occluding the relevant region, occluding an irrelevant one, and swapping in another patient's same-label scan, and combines three behavioral metrics to test whether a correct answer depends on the image. Across nine systems, a text-only model with no image access reaches within 5.7 accuracy points of the best multimodal one, and a 119-billion-parameter multimodal model is statistically indistinguishable from a 7-billion text-only baseline. The audit splits the cohort into three models that ignore the image, one that is unstable, and five that use it selectively, for a subset of findings; the categories hold across a second dataset, resolution, and prompt phrasing. Against board-certified radiologists, a text-only model is statistically indistinguishable from a radiologist's accuracy while grounding at zero, whereas the image-using models ground at radiologist-comparable rates. Reported confidence flags ungrounded answers only when a model uses the image. Grounding audits, not accuracy, should gate clinical deployment.
title: Vision-language models for chest radiography do not always need the image
title_translated: 用于胸部X光片的视觉-语言模型并不总是需要图像
summary_translated: 医学视觉-语言模型报告出较高的胸部X光片诊断准确率，这常被解读为它们实际使用了图像信息。这种推断并不安全：一个利用发现名称先验知识的模型表现得如同真正读取扫描图像的模型，而现有标准基准无法区分二者。我们引入一种因果审计方法，通过干预图像（遮蔽相关区域、遮蔽无关区域、替换为同标签其他患者的扫描图像），并结合三种行为指标测试正确答案是否依赖于图像。在九个系统中，完全无图像访问的纯文本模型准确率仅比最优多模态模型低5.7个百分点，而一个1190亿参数的多模态模型在统计上与70亿参数的纯文本基线模型无差异。该审计将样本分为三类：忽略图像的模型、不稳定模型、以及仅对部分发现使用图像的模型；这些分类在第二数据集、不同分辨率和提示表述下均保持一致。与持证放射科医生相比，无图像基础的纯文本模型在零基础情况下准确率与放射科医生无统计差异，而使用图像的模型则达到了与放射科医生相当的基础水平。报告的置信度标记仅在模型使用图像时才有效识别非基础回答。因此，应以基础性审计而非准确率作为临床部署的门槛。

summary: As LLM-based shopping agents enter production, existing benchmarks fail to capture how a shopper's requirements arrive: stated implicitly in the query, recorded in a profile, or revealed only when the right question is asked. Benchmarks that expose full intent upfront and grade only the final choice can neither pose this long-horizon challenge nor explain which requirement an agent missed. To address this gap, we introduce EComAgentBench, a benchmark of 662 tasks grounded in real Amazon products and reviews. Each task scatters these requirements across a visible query, a tool-gated profile, and scripted clarification; an agent must uncover hidden intent, verify candidates against attributes and review evidence, and commit to a single product within 100 tool calls. Moreover, typed, source-tagged rubrics grade every task, attributing each failure to a requirement and its source. Construction is automated yet reliable, with every answer fixed in code before any text is generated and every sample validated. Our evaluation of seven models reveals that even the strongest attains only 57.1% overall accuracy, and rubric satisfaction degrades from visible to hidden sources. Overall, we believe EComAgentBench will serve as a reproducible foundation for moving shopping agents from single-query search toward dependable assistance over long horizons.
title: EComAgentBench: Benchmarking Shopping Agents on Long-Horizon Tasks with Distributed Hidden Intent
title_translated: EComAgentBench：针对分布式隐藏意图的长周期任务购物智能体基准测试
summary_translated: 随着基于大语言模型的购物智能体进入生产阶段，现有基准无法捕捉购物者需求的呈现方式：需求可能隐含在查询中、记录在用户档案中，或仅在提出正确问题后才显现。那些提前暴露全部意图并仅根据最终选择评分的基准，既无法体现这种长周期挑战，也无法解释智能体遗漏了哪项需求。为填补这一空白，我们提出了EComAgentBench，一个包含662个任务的基准，其任务基于真实的亚马逊商品和评论构建。每个任务将需求分散在可见查询、工具控制的档案和脚本化澄清环节中；智能体必须挖掘隐藏意图，依据属性和评论证据验证候选商品，并在100次工具调用内选定唯一产品。此外，带有类型和来源标签的评分标准对每个任务进行评分，明确指出每次失败所对应的特定需求及其来源。该基准构建自动化且可靠，所有答案在文本生成前已固定于代码中，每条样本均经过验证。对七种模型的评估显示，即使最强模型的整体准确率也仅为57.1%，且评分满足度从显性来源向隐藏来源逐步下降。总体而言，我们认为EComAgentBench将成为推动购物智能体从单次查询搜索迈向长期可靠辅助的可复现基础。

summary: Reinforcement learning (RL) has emerged as a powerful paradigm for training Large Language Models (LLMs) as agents. However, conventional RL methods for long-horizon agentic tasks often struggle with sparse outcome rewards. Intuitively, this overlooks the rich environment dynamics information contained in rollout interaction trajectories. We argue that the interaction experience inherently serves as an implicit supervision signal, reveals the underlying transition mechanisms of the environment, and enables the agent to construct a more accurate internal model of the environment.. Therefore, in this work, we investigate how to leverage this additional signal to improve policy learning. Specifically, we propose EnvRL, a framework that incorporates environment dynamics learning into agentic RL via two auxiliary objectives: state prediction and inverse dynamics. By jointly optimizing with the primary RL objective, we encourage the agent to internalize environment dynamics from its own interaction experience. Extensive experiments on two long-horizon agentic benchmarks demonstrate that EnvRL achieves significant improvements on success-rates over RL-only baselines, e.g., when trained with GRPO, lifting Qwen-2.5-1.5B-Instruct from 72.8% to 77.4% on ALFWorld, and from 56.8% to 67.0% on WebShop.
title: EnvRL: Learn from Environment Dynamics in Agentic Reinforcement Learning
title_translated: EnvRL：在智能体强化学习中学习环境动态
summary_translated: 强化学习（RL）已成为训练大型语言模型（LLMs）作为智能体的强大范式。然而，传统的长周期智能体任务RL方法常面临稀疏结果奖励的问题。直观来看，这忽略了滚动交互轨迹中蕴含的丰富环境动态信息。我们认为，交互经验本身即构成一种隐式监督信号，揭示了环境的底层转移机制，并使智能体能够构建更准确的内部环境模型。因此，本文研究如何利用这一额外信号来改进策略学习。具体而言，我们提出EnvRL框架，通过两个辅助目标——状态预测和逆动力学——将环境动态学习融入智能体强化学习中。通过与主RL目标联合优化，鼓励智能体从自身交互经验中内化环境动态。在两个长周期智能体基准上的大量实验表明，EnvRL相较于仅使用RL的基线实现了显著提升，例如在GRPO训练下，Qwen-2.5-1.5B-Instruct在ALFWorld上的成功率从72.8%提升至77.4%，在WebShop上从56.8%提升至67.0%。

summary: Text-guided Open-vocabulary Object Counting (TOOC) aims to estimate the number of objects described by text prompts, which is particularly challenging in dense scenes with large scale variations. Existing TOOC approaches predominantly rely on Transformers, whose quadratic complexity with respect to image resolution limits their scalability. Mamba offers a promising alternative due to its linear complexity. However, previous Mamba-based methods have two main limitations. On the one hand, the inherent causal formulation of Mamba constrains the bidirectional spatial dependency modeling required by non-causal vision tasks. On the other hand, existing Mamba-based vision models often overlook the unconstrained high entropy in the spatial token responses, which can weaken local details and high-frequency cues. To address these limitations, we propose MambaCount, an efficient framework built on the Spatial Sparse State Space Duality (S^4D) block. Specifically, we analyze and reconstruct the decay dynamics of hidden states in Mamba to alleviate the dependency constraints introduced by causal modeling. Moreover, we introduce a Spatial Token Selection (STS) sub-block to reduce the unconstrained high entropy in spatial token responses within Mamba. In addition, we design Multi-Granularity Prototypes (MGP) to identify object-like regions at different semantic levels, improving cross-modal alignment and interpretability. Extensive experiments on FSC-147 demonstrate that MambaCount achieves state-of-the-art performance among methods without secondary querying, obtaining a test MAE of 12.23, while retaining linear complexity.
title: MambaCount: Efficient Text-guided Open-vocabulary Object Counting with Spatial Sparse State Space Duality Block
title_translated: MambaCount：基于空间稀疏状态空间对偶块的高效文本引导开放词汇目标计数
summary_translated: 文本引导开放词汇目标计数（TOOC）旨在估计由文本提示描述的对象数量，这在具有大规模变化的密集场景中尤为具有挑战性。现有TOOC方法主要依赖于Transformer，其与图像分辨率呈二次复杂度，限制了可扩展性。Mamba因其线性复杂度提供了有前景的替代方案。然而，先前基于Mamba的方法存在两个主要局限：一方面，Mamba固有的因果结构限制了非因果视觉任务所需的双向空间依赖建模；另一方面，现有的基于Mamba的视觉模型常忽略空间标记响应中的无约束高熵，这会削弱局部细节和高频线索。为解决这些局限，我们提出了MambaCount，一个基于空间稀疏状态空间对偶（S^4D）块的高效框架。具体而言，我们分析并重构了Mamba中隐藏状态的衰减动态，以缓解因果建模引入的依赖约束。此外，我们引入了空间标记选择（STS）子模块，以减少Mamba中空间标记响应的无约束高熵。同时，我们设计了多粒度原型（MGP），以在不同语义层次上识别类对象区域，提升跨模态对齐性和可解释性。在FSC-147上的大量实验表明，MambaCount在无需二次查询的方法中达到了最先进性能，测试MAE为12.23，同时保持线性复杂度。

summary: Large language model (LLM) web agents are usually deployed as tool callers: each turn, the model reads a fresh page observation and emits one structured tool action. When every action is a low-level primitive, horizons grow quickly and so do policy-facing LLM completions, dominating latency and cost on benchmarks such as Mind2Web and WebArena. Recent systems therefore wrap repeated interaction fragments as web skills: callable tools built from successful trajectories or induced programs, so one call can replace several primitives. However, prior skill libraries are still triggered mainly by instruction similarity or coarse site metadata, which yields low skill reuse on held-out sites and leaves much of the potential step and token reduction on the table.   We present SkillMigrator, an agent that learns reusable web skills and transfers them across sites by matching layout structure rather than specific element references. Each induced skill is stored as a transferable interaction pattern (TIP): the skill paired with a structural sketch of the snapshot at induction time. At test time, SkillMigrator retrieves TIPs by layout similarity and grounds their references on the live page. The rest of the stack is standard: accessibility-snapshot observations with stable references, and fixed tool calling over primitives plus skill invocations. Compared with the state-of-the-art approaches, SkillMigrator reduces the average LLM-action count on successful trajectories by 8-10% across both WebArena and Mind2Web at matched success rate.
title: Beyond Domains: Reusing Web Skills via Transferable Interaction Patterns
title_translated: 超越领域：通过可迁移交互模式复用网络技能
summary_translated: 大型语言模型（LLM）网络代理通常作为工具调用者部署：每一轮，模型读取新的页面观测并发出一个结构化工具动作。当每个动作都是低级原语时，决策范围迅速扩大，策略面向的LLM补全也随之增加，主导了Mind2Web和WebArena等基准测试中的延迟和成本。因此，近期系统将重复的交互片段封装为网络技能：由成功轨迹或推导程序构建的可调用工具，一次调用即可替代多个原语。然而，先前的技能库仍主要通过指令相似性或粗粒度站点元数据触发，导致在未见站点上的技能复用率较低，并未能充分实现步骤和令牌的减少潜力。我们提出SkillMigrator，一种能够学习可复用网络技能并在站点间迁移的智能体，其通过匹配布局结构而非特定元素引用实现迁移。每个生成的技能以可迁移交互模式（TIP）形式存储：技能与其诱导时刻快照的结构草图配对。测试时，SkillMigrator通过布局相似性检索TIP，并在实时页面上定位其引用。其余部分为标准架构：具有稳定引用的无障碍快照观测，以及固定原语补全加技能调用。与最先进方法相比，SkillMigrator在WebArena和Mind2Web上均以相同成功率将平均LLM动作数减少了8%-10%。

summary: Adding LLM-generated node features to graph neural networks (GNNs) is widely reported to improve accuracy on standard benchmarks. We document a contrasting observation: when LLM features are introduced through pure input concatenation (rather than joint training, distillation, or prompt-conditioning), they can systematically degrade accuracy on the same homophilous benchmarks where end-to-end LLM pipelines succeed. With an MLP backbone on the Planetoid public split and bag-of-words original features, concatenating SBERT-encoded GPT-4o-mini TAPE features reduces PubMed test accuracy by -17.0 +/- 0.3 pp and Cora by -4.3 +/- 0.6 pp (CiteSeer -0.6 +/- 0.8 pp, within seed noise). The drop attenuates as we relax each condition (GCN / GCNII / GAT backbones, random splits, smaller encoders) and reverses on medium-homophily WikiCS (+4.4 pp) and ogbn-arxiv (+11.7 pp). To predict when concatenation helps versus hurts, we report a simple measure of LLM-alone discriminability, Delta_sig. Across 9 datasets Delta_sig correlates with the concatenation cost more strongly than homophily at point estimate (r^2 = 0.38 vs. 0.06; N=9, bootstrap CIs overlap). The bootstrap-best change-point is tau = 13.8 pp, and the rule "Delta_sig <= tau predicts non-positive concat cost" classifies 7/9 datasets correctly; since 60% of bootstrap samples place tau in [5, 30] pp, we treat Delta_sig as an interpretive lens rather than a precision filter. A dimension-controlled ablation on PubMed places the LLM-feature drop between same-source PCA (-2.3 pp) and same-dim Gaussian noise (-37.3 pp), ruling out dimensionality and weight-decay artifacts. Nine PubMed configurations fit a power law |Delta_concat| proportional to (sqrt(d_l/n))^1.31 with r^2 = 0.97; the low-Delta_sig, small-n corner is exactly where the headline -17 pp PubMed deficit appears.
title: LLM Features Can Hurt GNNs: Concatenation Interference on Homophilous Graph Benchmarks
title_translated: LLM特征可能损害GNN：同质图基准测试中的拼接干扰
summary_translated: 将LLM生成的节点特征添加到图神经网络（GNN）中被广泛报道可提高标准基准测试的准确性。我们记录了一个相反的现象：当通过纯输入拼接方式引入LLM特征（而非联合训练、蒸馏或提示条件化）时，它们会在相同的同质基准测试中系统性地降低准确率，而端到端的LLM流水线在此类任务中表现良好。以Planetoid公开划分和词袋原始特征为基础，使用MLP主干网络，拼接SBERT编码的GPT-4o-mini TAPE特征使PubMed测试准确率下降-17.0±0.3个百分点，Cora下降-4.3±0.6个百分点（CiteSeer下降-0.6±0.8个百分点，处于种子噪声范围内）。随着条件放宽（GCN/GCNII/GAT主干、随机划分、更小编码器），下降幅度减弱，并在中等同质性WikiCS（+4.4个百分点）和ogbn-arxiv（+11.7个百分点）上逆转。为预测拼接何时有益或有害，我们报告了一种简单的LLM单独判别度度量Delta_sig。在9个数据集上，Delta_sig与拼接代价的相关性高于同质性（r²=0.38 vs. 0.06；N=9，Bootstrap置信区间重叠）。Bootstrap最优拐点为tau=13.8个百分点，“Delta_sig ≤ tau”规则正确分类7/9个数据集；由于60%的Bootstrap样本将tau置于[5,30]区间内，我们将Delta_sig视为解释性视角而非精确过滤器。在PubMed上的维度控制消融实验表明，LLM特征下降介于同源PCA（-2.3个百分点）与同维高斯噪声（-37.3个百分点）之间，排除了维度和权重衰减的伪影。九个PubMed配置符合幂律|Delta_concat| ∝ (sqrt(d_l/n))^1.31，r²=0.97；低Delta_sig、小n的角落正是-17个百分点的显著缺陷出现的位置。

summary: Non-autoregressive (NAR) decoding generates output tokens in parallel, making speech recognition faster than autoregressive decoding, which generates them sequentially from left to right. However, the recognition performance is degraded because NAR decoding cannot resolve uncertainty by conditioning on previously generated tokens. To address this issue, we propose a novel NAR decoding framework based on minimum Bayes' risk (MBR) decoding, termed NAR-MBR decoding, that maximizes the expected utility calculated from samples drawn from the output probability of an NAR model rather than maximizing the output probability. Notably, by leveraging the nature of NAR models, multiple samples are obtained efficiently with a single forward computation. Our experiments across LibriSpeech, Switchboard, AMI, and web presentation corpus demonstrated that our NAR-MBR decoding outperformed previous NAR decoding and ran faster than AR decoding.
title: Non-Autoregressive Minimum Bayes' Risk Decoding for Fast Speech Recognition
title_translated: 非自回归最小贝叶斯风险解码用于快速语音识别
summary_translated: 非自回归（NAR）解码以并行方式生成输出标记，使语音识别速度优于自回归解码（从左到右顺序生成）。然而，由于NAR解码无法通过依赖先前生成的标记来消除不确定性，识别性能会下降。为解决此问题，我们提出一种基于最小贝叶斯风险（MBR）解码的新NAR解码框架，称为NAR-MBR解码，它最大化从NAR模型输出概率中抽取样本所计算的期望效用，而非最大化输出概率本身。值得注意的是，利用NAR模型的特性，可通过单次前向计算高效获取多个样本。我们在LibriSpeech、Switchboard、AMI和网络演示语料库上的实验表明，我们的NAR-MBR解码优于以往的NAR解码方法，并且运行速度超过自回归解码。

summary: Prompt injection defenses evaluated on synthetic benchmarks do not generalize to real enterprise documents, which are longer, denser, and interleave legitimate authority language with factual content. We demonstrate this gap with a real-document benchmark of 122 tasks across five professional domains (financial, legal, medical, scientific, DevOps) using actual SEC filings, Federal Register rules, PubMed abstracts, arXiv papers, and GitHub postmortems. Paraphrasing, the strongest defense on synthetic benchmarks, shows no statistically significant attack success rate reduction on real documents (p=0.500) while degrading utility from 91.8% to 82.8%. We introduce PARSE (Provenance-Aware Retrieval Sanitization), a domain-aware, fact-preserving sanitization pipeline that classifies each sentence by injection likelihood, extracts structured facts before rewriting, and verifies fact preservation via a consistency-checking loop. A directiveness gate routes 59% of real enterprise documents to a lightweight path, concentrating computational cost on high-risk documents. PARSE achieves 15.6% attack success rate -- a 38% reduction versus the 25.4% baseline -- at 86.9% utility, the only condition that is both statistically significant (p=0.014, adequately powered) and maintains near-baseline utility. Practitioners should evaluate defenses on domain-matched real documents, not synthetic proxies.
title: PARSE: Provenance-Aware Retrieval Sanitization for Professional Domain LLM Agents
comment: 7 pages, 3 figures, 2 tables. Under submission at EMNLP 2026 Industry Track
title_translated: PARSE：面向专业领域LLM代理的溯源感知检索净化
summary_translated: 在合成基准测试上评估的提示注入防御无法推广到真实企业文档，因为真实文档更长、更密集，并且合法权威语言与事实内容交织在一起。我们通过一个包含122个任务的实证文档基准测试验证了这一差距，涵盖五个专业领域（金融、法律、医疗、科学、DevOps），使用实际的SEC文件、联邦公报规则、PubMed摘要、arXiv论文和GitHub事后分析报告。在合成基准测试中最强的防御方法——改写（paraphrasing），在真实文档上并未显著降低攻击成功率（p=0.500），同时导致实用性从91.8%下降至82.8%。我们提出PARSE（溯源感知检索净化），一种领域感知、事实保留的净化管道，能够按注入可能性对每个句子进行分类，提取结构化事实后再重写，并通过一致性检查循环验证事实保留。一个直接性门控将59%的真实企业文档路由至轻量路径，将计算成本集中于高风险文档。PARSE实现了15.6%的攻击成功率——比25.4%的基线降低了38%，同时保持86.9%的实用性，这是唯一既具统计显著性（p=0.014，充分功率）又接近基线实用性的条件。从业者应使用领域匹配的真实文档评估防御措施，而非合成代理。

summary: Large language models (LLMs) are becoming a major way for consumers to find products, but we do not yet understand how brands compete in this new channel. We study brand dynamics in LLM recommendations using skincare products -- a category where consumers cannot easily judge quality before buying and must rely on brand reputation -- across three commercial LLMs (GPT-4o-mini, Claude Sonnet, Gemini 3 Flash), with a robustness check on search goods. In three experiments, we find: (1) a Conditional Monopoly where well-known brands get recommended 100% of the time (IAI = 10.0) when all products have the same specifications, but this dominance disappears with less than a +0.1-star rating advantage for a competitor; (2) authority-style marketing language, including fabricated clinical-evidence claims, breaks this monopoly at a Bias Surplus Value equal to +0.17 rating points, with each model responding differently; and (3) a social dilemma in multi-brand GEO competition: when all brands adopt the same optimization strategy, individual payoff falls from +0.802 to +0.007 in our payoff proxy, and non-participating brands receive zero recommendations in our tests. Our results suggest that generative engine optimization (GEO) should be studied not only as a security risk, but also as an emerging marketing practice that shapes market competition.
title: Incumbent Advantage: Brand Bias and Cognitive Manipulation Dynamics in LLM Recommendation Systems
title_translated: incumbent优势：大语言模型推荐系统中的品牌偏见与认知操控机制
summary_translated: 大型语言模型（LLMs）正成为消费者发现产品的主要方式，但我们尚不清楚品牌在此新渠道中的竞争机制。我们通过护肤产品这一类别（消费者在购买前难以判断质量，必须依赖品牌声誉）研究了LLM推荐中的品牌动态，涵盖三种商用LLM（GPT-4o-mini、Claude Sonnet、Gemini 3 Flash），并以搜索品进行了稳健性检验。在三个实验中，我们发现：（1）当所有产品规格相同时，知名品牌会获得100%的推荐（IAI = 10.0），形成条件垄断；但只要竞争对手有超过+0.1星的评分优势，这种主导地位即消失；（2）权威式营销语言（包括虚构的临床证据声明）可打破这一垄断，其偏见盈余价值相当于+0.17分评分点，且各模型反应不同；（3）在多品牌地理竞争中存在社会困境：当所有品牌采用相同优化策略时，个体收益从+0.802降至+0.007（以收益代理衡量），未参与的品牌在测试中则完全得不到推荐。结果表明，生成引擎优化（GEO）不仅应被视为安全风险，也应作为塑造市场竞争的新营销实践加以研究。

summary: Multimodal Foundation Models are increasingly used as reasoning agents, making reliability, knowing when a model may hallucinate, critical. A common intuition, which we call the Attention-Confidence Assumption, holds that reliability follows from "structural" visual perception: tight attention on relevant regions should signal a trustworthy answer, while scattered attention signals confusion. We challenge this through the VLM Reliability Probe (VRP), a systematic cross-family study of reliability signals in contemporary Vision-Language Models (VLMs). We introduce structural-attention metrics, cluster counts (C_k) and spatial entropy (H_s), to quantify the visual encoder's gaze, and track its evolution (Delta H_s) across layers. This reveals a "Symbolic Detachment": models often "Early Lock" visual features only to diffuse attention later, severing early perception from final generation. Contrary to the grounding hypothesis, we find a "Cluster Failure": spatial attention has near-zero correlation (R approx 0.001) with accuracy. Instead, reliability is a phenomenon of generation dynamics and internal-state distributions. Self-Consistency, the agreement rate across sampled reasoning paths, is the dominant predictor of truth (R = 0.429). Scaling causal interventions exposes a sharp architectural divergence: LLaVA locks its prediction in a fragile late-stage bottleneck, whereas PaliGemma and Qwen2-VL distribute reliability globally, staying resilient even when ~50% or more of their most predictive layer is destroyed. For current VLMs, reliability signals are detached from visual grounding maps and are best inferred from generation-time dynamics and hidden-state probes.
title: Visuals Lie, Consistency Speaks: Disentangling Spatial Attention from Reliability in Vision-Language Models
comment: 16 pages. Accepted to the ICLR 2026 Workshop on Multimodal Intelligence. Code: this https URL
title_translated: 视觉说谎，一致性发声：解耦视觉-语言模型中的空间注意力与可靠性
summary_translated: 多模态基础模型越来越多地被用作推理智能体，因此其可靠性——即模型何时可能产生幻觉——变得至关重要。一种普遍直觉（我们称之为‘注意力-信心假设’）认为，可靠性源于‘结构化’的视觉感知：对相关区域的紧密关注应表明可信答案，而分散注意力则暗示困惑。我们通过VLM可靠性探测器（VRP）对当代视觉-语言模型（VLMs）的可靠性信号进行系统性跨家族研究，挑战了这一观点。我们引入结构化注意力度量指标，如聚类数（C_k）和空间熵（H_s），以量化视觉编码器的注视行为，并追踪其在各层间的演化（Delta H_s）。结果显示存在‘符号脱离’现象：模型常早期锁定视觉特征，随后扩散注意力，使早期感知与最终生成脱节。与根基假说相反，我们发现‘聚类失败’：空间注意力与准确率几乎无相关性（R ≈ 0.001）。相反，可靠性是生成动态和内部状态分布的现象。自一致性（多次采样推理路径的一致率）是预测真实性的主要指标（R = 0.429）。因果干预的扩展揭示了显著的架构差异：LLaVA在其脆弱的后期瓶颈中锁定预测，而PaliGemma和Qwen2-VL则将可靠性全局分布，即使约50%或更多的最具预测力层被破坏仍保持鲁棒性。对于当前的VLM，可靠性信号与视觉根基图分离，最佳推断方式来自生成过程动态和隐藏状态探测。

summary: Speech offers a uniquely informative window into health by simultaneously engaging neurological, motor, respiratory, and vocal systems. Current clinical speech AI methods have largely progressed through isolated condition-specific studies, making results difficult to compare and generalization difficult to assess. We introduce SpeechDx, a large-scale benchmark for clinical speech AI spanning 12 datasets and 27 tasks across diverse health conditions. To enable evaluation across shared clinical mechanisms, SpeechDx structures tasks by the stage of speech production they disrupt: conceptualization, formulation, and articulation. The benchmark tests generalization by including tasks with limited labeled data and evaluating the same health condition across multiple datasets, distinguishing clinically meaningful patterns from dataset artefacts. We systematically evaluate 12 state-of-the-art audio encoders across all tasks and under zero-shot cross-condition transfer. Results show that large-scale speech models represent the strongest overall baselines, domain-specific models improve performance only on closely matched tasks, and no current representation generalizes reliably across the clinical speech landscape. SpeechDx establishes a shared evaluation framework for tracking progress toward general-purpose clinical speech representations
title: SpeechDx: A Multi-Task Benchmark for Clinical Speech AI
title_translated: SpeechDx：临床语音人工智能的多任务基准
summary_translated: 语音通过同时激活神经、运动、呼吸和发声系统，为健康提供了独特而丰富的信息窗口。当前临床语音AI方法大多基于孤立的特定疾病研究，导致结果难以比较，泛化能力难以评估。我们提出SpeechDx，一个大规模临床语音AI基准，涵盖12个数据集和27项任务，覆盖多种健康状况。为实现对共享临床机制的评估，SpeechDx按语音生成阶段中断的类型组织任务：概念化、构想和发音。该基准通过包含标注数据有限的任务以及在多个数据集中评估同一健康状况来测试泛化能力，以区分临床有意义模式与数据集伪影。我们系统评估了12种最先进的音频编码器在所有任务上的表现，并在零样本跨疾病迁移条件下进行测试。结果表明，大规模语音模型代表最强的整体基线，领域特定模型仅在任务高度匹配时提升性能，而当前任何表示都无法在临床语音领域可靠泛化。SpeechDx建立了一个共享评估框架，用于跟踪通用临床语音表示的发展进展。

summary: AI systems based on artificial neural networks are being developed with aspirations of pushing the boundary of human mathematical knowledge. A key question for these systems is how much they can reach beyond their training data. Mathematical discovery requires a strong form of out of distribution generalization; the ability to hypothesize genuinely new - and potentially logically more powerful - mathematical structures. It has been hypothesized that language abilities support such generalizations in human cognition. In this work, we use simple arithmetic as a case study for examining how modern AI models could expand their mathematical horizons, evaluating whether these models can independently discover the concept of "zero". We show that We show that (1) language models of a GPT-2 size are unable to perform this generalization at test time regardless of language pretraining, but (2) models can improve substantially after training on tens or hundreds of examples of zero. Additionally, we find that language pretraining reduces the number of required examples by approximately $50\%$, showing that language abilities can scaffold mathematical discovery in neural models.
title: Nothing from Something: Can a Language Model Discover 0?
title_translated: 从无到有：语言模型能否发现‘零’？
summary_translated: 基于人工神经网络的AI系统正被开发以突破人类数学知识的边界。这些系统面临的一个关键问题是它们能超越训练数据的程度。数学发现需要强形式的分布外泛化能力，即能够提出真正新颖且可能逻辑上更强大的数学结构。已有假设认为语言能力支持人类认知中的此类泛化。本研究以简单算术为例，探讨现代AI模型如何拓展其数学视野，评估这些模型是否能独立发现‘零’的概念。我们发现：（1）GPT-2规模的语言模型无论经过何种语言预训练，在测试时均无法完成此泛化；（2）但在接受数十或数百个关于‘零’的例子训练后，模型性能可显著提升。此外，我们发现语言预训练可使所需示例数量减少约50%，表明语言能力可在神经模型中支撑数学发现。

summary: Reinforcement learning (RL) has become a central paradigm for post-training large language models. Existing critic-free RL methods typically generate a group of rollouts for the same question to estimate value baselines for advantage computation. However, this design suffers from data inefficiency, group synchronization barriers, and inflexibility with structured rollouts. In this work, we revisit the role of the ``group'' and show that its underlying function is not merely to estimate baselines but to prevent false penalties on negative samples. Building on this insight, we propose negative token filtering, a simple and effective strategy that enables stable single-rollout training. We apply it to two batch-level advantage methods, achieving comparable performance on reasoning tasks and stronger performance on agentic tasks relative to group-based RL techniques.
title: Rethinking Groups in Critic-Free RLVR
title_translated: 重新思考无评判者强化学习中的群体机制
summary_translated: 强化学习（RL）已成为大语言模型后训练的核心范式。现有无评判者RL方法通常为同一问题生成一组轨迹以估计价值基线，用于优势计算。然而，这种设计存在数据效率低、群体同步障碍以及对结构化轨迹灵活性不足的问题。本文重新审视‘群体’的作用，指出其根本功能并非仅估计基线，而是防止对负样本施加错误惩罚。基于此洞察，我们提出负标记过滤策略，一种简单有效的单轨迹训练方法。我们将该策略应用于两种批处理优势方法，在推理任务上达到相当性能，并在代理任务上优于基于群体的RL技术。

summary: A model that lies while knowing the truth is the central case ELK cannot handle with behavioral evaluation alone. We ask whether such deception leaves an internal signature distinguishing it from honest error. Our key move is a control for wrongness: we contrast a sleeper agent (knows the truth, lies on trigger) against a naive liar (fine-tuned to emit the same wrong answers with no honest training). Both produce identical wrong outputs; any difference is about knowledge conflict, not incorrectness. We find deceptive forward passes carry a conflict signature - 2.1-2.3x higher residual rank than naive-liar passes on the same wrong answer - strong enough to identify which of two responses is the lie with 100% accuracy and no labels, across GPT-2 small/medium (three seeds) and three instruct models. Across Qwen2.5-1.5B/7B and Phi-3-mini, instructed deception raises residual rank on every tested fact (18/18, 40/40, 34/34); on Phi-3, lies separate perfectly from both honest answers and hallucinations (AUC 1.0, Wilcoxon p~6e-11). The signature survives strategic self-constructed deception (model invents its own lie, AUC 1.0), active concealment attempts (AUC 1.0), and length-controlled replication (20/20, AUC 1.0, p~1e-6). Using basis-free relative representations, a probe trained on one model family detects deception in two other families zero-shot (mean AUC 0.933), surviving simultaneous architecture and format change (AUC 0.821), and transfers across five languages (AUC 1.000, length-controlled). The signature is read-only: detectable but not injectable (0/8 both directions). Honest limitations and six negative experiments are documented in full.
title: Rift: A Conflict Signature for Deception in Language Models
comment: 13 pages, 4 figures. Code and experiment logs: this https URL
title_translated: Rift：语言模型欺骗行为的冲突签名
summary_translated: 一个明知真相却说谎的模型是ELK无法仅通过行为评估处理的核心案例。我们探讨这种欺骗是否会在内部留下可区分于诚实错误的特征信号。关键思路是控制错误性：将‘潜伏代理’（知道真相但触发时说谎）与‘天真说谎者’（经过微调以产生相同错误答案，无诚实训练）进行对比。两者输出完全相同的错误结果；任何差异均源于知识冲突而非错误本身。我们发现，欺骗性的前向传播具有显著的冲突签名——在相同错误答案下，其残差秩比天真说谎者的传播高出2.1至2.3倍，足以在无标签情况下以100%准确率识别两个响应中哪个是谎言，涵盖GPT-2 small/medium（三个种子）和三种指令模型。在Qwen2.5-1.5B/7B和Phi-3-mini上，指令式欺骗在所有测试事实中均提升残差秩（18/18, 40/40, 34/34）；在Phi-3上，谎言与诚实回答及幻觉完美分离（AUC 1.0，Wilcoxon p~6e-11）。该签名在策略性自构建欺骗（模型自行发明谎言，AUC 1.0）、主动隐藏尝试（AUC 1.0）和长度控制复制（20/20，AUC 1.0，p~1e-6）中依然有效。使用无基底相对表示，一个在某一模型家族上训练的探测器可在零样本条件下检测另外两个家族中的欺骗（平均AUC 0.933），并经受住同时的架构与格式变化（AUC 0.821）以及跨五种语言迁移（AUC 1.000，长度控制）。该签名为只读：可检测但不可注入（0/8双向）。诚实局限性和六项负实验已完整记录。

summary: Current multilingual evaluations for Vision-Language Models (VLMs) assume a one-to-one mapping between language and orthography, overlooking billions of users of multi-script languages. We introduce PuMVR (Punjabi Multimodal Visual Reasoning), a benchmark of 1,000 strictly parallel image-text instances across Punjabi's three active scripts: Gurmukhi, Shahmukhi, and Roman. Evaluating 10 state-of-the-art VLMs, we expose a substantial and systematic Script Gap. Models frequently solve visual tasks in one script while failing identical tasks in another, with accuracy deltas reaching 16%. Crucially, visual input boosts absolute performance uniformly yet does not close the orthographic gap. Furthermore, cross-script in-context transfer is highly brittle, exposing script-locked knowledge representation. Supported by McNemar tests across all script pairs, our findings demonstrate that current "multilingual" VLMs are not truly multi-script. We propose the Script Consistency Rate (SCR), which falls as low as 24.8% on our benchmark, as a mandatory metric for script-agnostic evaluation to ensure equitable AI access. Data and code are available at: https://github.com/prabhjotschugh/Not-Truly-Multilingual-PuMVR.
title: Not Truly Multilingual: Script Consistency as a Missing Dimension in VLM Evaluation
title_translated: 并非真正多语言：脚本一致性作为视觉-语言模型评估中缺失的维度
summary_translated: 当前针对视觉-语言模型（VLMs）的多语言评估假设语言与书写系统之间存在一一对应关系，忽略了数十亿使用多书写系统语言的用户。我们提出了PuMVR（旁遮普语多模态视觉推理）基准，包含1000个严格平行的图像-文本实例，覆盖旁遮普语的三种活跃书写系统：古鲁穆基、沙姆基和罗马字母。对10个最先进的VLMs进行评估后，我们揭示了显著且系统性的‘脚本差距’。模型在一种书写系统中能解决视觉任务，但在另一种书写系统中却失败，准确率差异高达16%。关键的是，视觉输入虽普遍提升了绝对性能，但并未弥合书写系统间的差距。此外，跨书写系统的上下文内迁移极为脆弱，暴露出书写系统锁定的知识表征。通过所有书写系统配对的McNemar检验支持，我们的研究证明当前所谓的‘多语言’VLMs并非真正多书写系统。我们提出脚本一致性率（SCR），在我们的基准上最低可达24.8%，作为实现书写系统无关评估的强制性指标，以确保人工智能的公平访问。数据与代码见：https://github.com/prabhjotschugh/Not-Truly-Multilingual-PuMVR。
